# Process Flow

This page explains how one user message becomes a structured model turn.

## Input Normalization

`utils/processUserInput/processUserInput.ts` handles the first major transformation. It accepts raw text or content blocks and turns them into a richer internal representation with attachments, slash commands, hooks, and metadata.

Important responsibilities:
- show the user prompt immediately while processing continues
- expand pasted content and image attachments
- recognize slash commands unless the input is explicitly protected
- apply submit hooks and stop the turn if a hook blocks it
- attach extra context if a hook provides it

## Why This Layer Exists

The agent needs to distinguish between literal chat text, commands, files, and structured system-generated inputs. Doing this before the query engine keeps the downstream turn loop simpler and makes hook behavior consistent.

## Hook Boundary

User-prompt submit hooks can:
- block execution with a warning
- stop continuation while keeping the prompt in context
- inject extra attachment context
- modify the resulting message stream

This makes the turn loop extensible without hardcoding policy in the query engine.
